---
title: "lims-core"
subtitle: "A Laboratory Information Management System where the audit trail, e-signatures, and chain of custody are properties of the database."
---

lims-core is an open-source LIMS for clinical research: biospecimen and biobank
management for trials first, on a domain-neutral core designed to also serve
analytical labs. It is the third sibling to
[edc-core](https://github.com/tgerke/edc-core) (Electronic Data Capture) and
[ctms-core](https://github.com/tgerke/ctms-core) (Clinical Trial Management);
the three are converging onto one interoperable platform.

![A sample record: storage, chain of custody, DataMatrix label, and a versioned, verified, e-signed result.](images/04-sample-detail.png){.screenshot fig-alt="lims-core sample detail page showing storage location, custody events, a DataMatrix label, and a signed result"}

::: {.callout-important}
## Where this is in its life

lims-core is at its first end-to-end milestone: one workflow, working
front-to-back, on a production-shaped compliance core. It is a strong
foundation and a working demo, **not yet a production system** for running a
regulated biobank, and **not a drop-in replacement for a commercial LIMS**.
The honest, detailed gap analysis is in the
[completeness review](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core/blob/main/docs/completeness-review.md)
— read it before planning any deployment.
:::

## Three design commitments

1. **Compliance as schema, not as feature.** The append-only audit trail is
   hash-chained and written by database triggers; results are versioned rows
   that can never be overwritten; signatures are cryptographically bound to
   the exact record version they approve. The application connects as a
   least-privilege role that cannot alter any of this — a bug or a rushed
   hotfix has no path around the controls.

2. **A domain-neutral core.** Samples, tests, results, storage, and custody
   are modeled once. The biobank workflow ships first; the analytical-lab
   layer (test specifications, QC, certificates of analysis) is designed as a
   follow-on module on the same core, not a rewrite.

3. **References, never PHI.** A specimen links to the EDC subject it was
   collected from by reference only. No patient health information enters the
   LIMS; the subject-data boundary between the siblings is architecture, not
   configuration.

## What works today

One specimen travels from receipt to a signed, verifiable result: accession →
DataMatrix label → freezer storage → test order → result entry → four-eyes
verification → e-signature with password step-up → a hash-chained audit trail
you can verify with one click. All of it runs front-to-back with OIDC or
password auth and grant-based access scoped to study and site.

## Where to start

| If you are… | Start here |
| --- | --- |
| Trying the app on your machine | [Getting started](getting-started.qmd) — running locally, with seeded demo accounts. |
| Working in the lab (or evaluating the workflow) | [User guide](user-guide/index.qmd) — the app, task by task, with screenshots. |
| Assessing the compliance story | [Compliance](compliance.qmd) — what is enforced, and the test that proves each claim. |
| Sizing the gap to production | [Roadmap and status](roadmap.qmd) — what exists, what is designed, what is not started. |

: {tbl-colwidths="[35,65]"}

lims-core is licensed **AGPL-3.0-only**. Source, decisions, and internal
architecture docs live [on GitHub](https://github.com/tgerke/lims-core).
